functions.name: assign_column
functions.description: Create a new column in a DataFrame based on the given expression.

arguments.name: column_name
arguments.description: The name of the new column.

arguments.name: expression
arguments.description: The expression used to create the new column.

databases.name: Data to use
databases.description: The DataFrame to which a new column will be added.

functions.name: calculate_age
functions.description: Calculate the calendar age in years from the birthdate column to a reference date (defaults to today).

arguments.name: column
arguments.description: The name of the column containing birthdate information.

arguments.name: output_column
arguments.description: The name of the column to store the age.

arguments.name: fmt
arguments.description: The format string to parse the birthdate column. If None, the format

arguments.name: keep_original
arguments.description: Whether to keep the original birthdate column in the DataFrame.

arguments.name: reference_date
arguments.description: The date to use as the reference for calculating age. If None, today's

functions.name: change_column_type
functions.description: Change DataFrame column data types.

arguments.name: columns
arguments.description: List of column names whose data types you want to change.

arguments.name: target_type
arguments.description: The desired data type for the specified columns.

databases.name: Data to use
databases.description: The DataFrame whose columns' data types you want to change.

functions.name: collapse
functions.description: Collapses a DataFrame by grouping by one or more columns and aggregating the rest.

arguments.name: group_columns
arguments.description: Columns by which the DataFrame will be grouped.

arguments.name: single_aggregation_strategy
arguments.description: Aggregate all columns using the same strategy if enabled. Otherwise, provide a dictionary with strategy per column.

arguments.name: aggregation_strategy
arguments.description: The aggregation strategy to apply. This strategy is applied to all columns. If

arguments.description: A dictionary for specific manner of  aggregation per column. Each column can

arguments.name: default_aggregation
arguments.description: Used for left-over columns when column-specific aggregation is

arguments.name: strict_mode
arguments.description: If True, all columns not in groupby must have an aggregation

functions.name: discretize_column
functions.description: Discretize a column in a new DataFrame based on the given bin edges or number of bins.

arguments.name: column_name
arguments.description: The name of the column to discretize.

arguments.name: bins
arguments.description: The bins to use in the discretization.

arguments.name: labels
arguments.description: Labels to assign to the bins.

arguments.name: right
arguments.description: Indicates whether bins include the rightmost edge or not.

arguments.name: include_lowest
arguments.description: Whether the first interval should include the lowest value or not.

arguments.name: output_column
arguments.description: The name of the output column that contains the discretized data.

databases.name: Data to use
databases.description: The DataFrame from which a new DataFrame will be created.

functions.name: drop_columns
functions.description: Drop specified columns from the DataFrame.

arguments.name: columns
arguments.description: A list of column names to be dropped from the DataFrame.

databases.name: Data to use
databases.description: The DataFrame from which columns will be dropped.

functions.name: encode

arguments.name: mapping
arguments.description: Dictionary containing the mapping for encoding. Keys are original

arguments.name: unknown_value
arguments.description: Value to use for any unknown categories.

arguments.name: unknown_value_type
arguments.description: The type of the unknown value. Can be "str" or "int".

arguments.name: raise_on_unknown
arguments.description: If True, raises an error when encountering an unknown category.

functions.name: extract_from_string
functions.description: Extracts specific patterns from a string column in a DataFrame.

arguments.name: not_found
arguments.description: The value to insert if the pattern is not found.

arguments.name: keep_original
arguments.description: If True, retains the original column. If False, removes the original

arguments.name: output_column
arguments.description: The name for the new column containing extracted data.

functions.name: group_statistics

arguments.description: The columns by which the DataFrame will be grouped.

arguments.name: prefix
arguments.description: An optional prefix for the names of new columns.

functions.name: impute

arguments.description: List of maximum values for scaling; should match the length of columns.

arguments.description: List of columns to scale; if None, all columns are scaled and the

functions.name: one_hot_encode
functions.description: Perform one-hot encoding on a specific column of a DataFrame.

arguments.name: column
arguments.description: The column to one-hot encode.

arguments.name: categories
arguments.description: List of predefined categories.

arguments.description: Label for unseen categories.

arguments.name: drop_original
arguments.description: Whether to drop the original column, default is True.

arguments.name: prefix
arguments.description: Prefix for the new one-hot encoded columns.
